BLEND+ · Company Overview
It doesn't exist.
And it's a great idea.
An autonomous nutrition machine that makes personalized protein shakes, energy drinks, and hydration blends in under 90 seconds. Thousands of combinations. Organic ingredients. Built-in member retention. Revenue for the host. Zero cost to place.

The Three-Layer Moat
01 · Reliable automation
Allen-Bradley PLC built by Water Innovations — a 17-year industrial automation integrator. 99.5% uptime target. NSF/ANSI 25 + ETL certified.
02 · Sticky behavior software
Loyalty points, streaks, tier system, dynamic pricing, and Happy Hour mechanics. Every mechanic drives repeat behavior and is measurable from the dashboard.
03 · Host relationship network
A host who has seen their revenue share arrive on time for two years is a host who will not seriously consider a competing pitch.

SAFE Structure
No interest. No maturity date. No forced conversion event. Converts to equity at the next priced round at the $3M cap or the round price minus 20% discount — whichever is more favorable to the investor. YC SAFE template. One page.

BLEND+ · Exit Thesis
This category resolves
through acquisition.
f'real was acquired by Rich Products. Chowbotics was acquired by DoorDash. The supplement company acquirer acquires BLEND+ for its distribution network, its behavioral data, and its machine fleet.
Most Likely Acquirer Profile
  • Supplement company seeking gym-floor distribution — Glanbia (Optimum Nutrition) is the primary candidate
  • Gym chain seeking vertical integration of nutrition revenue
  • Commercial food equipment rollup — Middleby Corporation as reference model
  • Private equity seeking recurring revenue in the wellness infrastructure category
The Glanbia Thesis
Glanbia owns Optimum Nutrition — the largest protein supplement brand in the US. At 100 machines with $11M annual gross revenue and an established gym host network, BLEND+ represents a distribution channel Glanbia does not have and cannot build as cheaply as acquiring it. Every purchase order BLEND+ sends to Glanbia Nutritionals for ingredient supply is a data point that says "this company moves product through gyms at scale."
What Makes BLEND+ Valuable at Exit
Fleet + host network
100+ machines deployed in gyms across the country with host relationships that took years to build. Distribution that cannot be bought off a shelf.
Behavioral data
Every order logged — what members buy, when, how often, what supplements they add. A dataset no supplement company currently has at the point of consumption.
Recurring revenue
$11M+ annual gross at 100 machines, growing with every deployment. Predictable, recurring, high-margin. Exactly what an acquirer pays a premium multiple for.
BLEND+ · The Machine
Sealed. Autonomous.
Fully managed.
Single-lane architecture. Funnel chamber. 12 HDPE cartridge hoppers. Allen-Bradley PLC. NSF/ANSI 25 + ETL certified pathway via Intertek.
Machine Specifications
  • Single production lane — one blend chamber, one dispense station
  • 12 HDPE cartridge hoppers — RFID verified, load cell confirmed, encoder tracked
  • Funnel chamber — wide receiving cone + narrow blend cylinder
  • Silverson BE275 bottom-entry rotor/stator — 3,000–15,000 RPM variable
  • Allen-Bradley CompactLogix PLC — full state machine control
  • Scotsman UF0915 flake ice maker — 96 lb/day, NSF-12
  • 5-stage RO water system — sub-10 ppm TDS
  • 360° CIP spray ball — hot water + steam + UV-C after every order
  • Off-hours extended CIP — 11pm and 4:30am daily
  • 99.5% uptime target — 155 min max downtime per month
Certifications
NSF/ANSI 25ETL ListedIntertek PathwaySan Diego DEHHACCP Plan
Single Lane Architecture — Why
  • Shared manifold in dual-lane creates cross-contamination risk — eliminated by going single lane
  • At 44 orders/day across 17 operating hours, one lane has ample throughput capacity
  • Simpler machine = higher reliability. Every component removed is a failure mode eliminated
  • BOM savings: ~$3,000–$6,000 per machine versus dual-lane architecture
  • CIP cycle runs after every order — 90 seconds total including full manifold clean and air purge
BLEND+ · Consumer App
Built and live.
Members order from their phone, customize their drink, pay with Apple Pay or card, and pick up in under 90 seconds. Full loyalty ecosystem built in from day one.
Home
Order
Build Your Shake
Rewards
Blend Bucks
Earned on every purchase. Redeemable for free drinks, upgrades, and add-ons. The more they spend, the more they earn.
Tier System
Bronze → Silver → Gold → Platinum. Higher tiers earn faster, unlock free perks, and get exclusive access. Silver trial for every new member.
Happy Hour
Dynamic pricing windows drive off-peak volume. 20% off during defined windows. Configurable per location from the operator dashboard.

BLEND+ · Operator Dashboard
Full transparency.
Real-time.
Every transaction logged as it happens. Revenue, orders, member behavior, machine status, projected payout — visible from any device at any time.
What Hosts See
  • Orders, revenue, customers — by day, week, month, year
  • Month-over-month growth with % change
  • Projected payout based on current run rate
  • Next payment date
  • Peak hours and slowest hours of the day
  • Top 5 most ordered drinks
  • Member rating and repeat customer rate
Operator-Only View
  • Ingredient levels per hopper — percentage remaining
  • CO₂ canister weight — low alert threshold
  • Cup stack count — refill alert
  • Water TDS reading — filter quality
  • Machine uptime and outage history
  • CIP cycle completion log
  • Remote door unlock override
BLEND+ · Product Formulation
Organic. Verified.
Personalized.
All organic except the rotating fun flavor and creatine HCl. No artificial ingredients in core lineup. Full nutrition transparency via QR code on every cup.
12-Hopper Configuration
HoppersCategoryContentsType
1–3BasesWhole Milk · Oat Milk · Coconut MilkPermanent · Organic
4–6Core ProteinsVanilla Grass-Fed Whey · Chocolate Grass-Fed Whey · Mixed Berry PlantPermanent · Organic

9SupplementElectrolytes — organic blendPermanent · Organic
10SupplementAmino Acids (EAAs)Permanent
11SupplementLumati Gold — clinical formulaPermanent · Clinical

Vanilla Whey

Organic pea protein · Organic brown rice protein · Freeze-dried strawberry · Freeze-dried blueberry · Freeze-dried tart cherry · Sunflower lecithin · Organic vanilla (0.3%)
Rotating Flavor Pipeline

  • Mint Chocolate Chip — post-workout refresh
  • Cinnamon Roll — morning window, trending
  • Horchata — San Diego cultural resonance
  • Matcha — premium wellness tier

BLEND+ · Machine Architecture
Single lane.
Funnel chamber.
One powder path, one blend chamber, one dispense station. No shared manifold. No diverter valve. Maximum sanitation, maximum reliability.
Powder Path — Cartridge to Cup
  • HDPE cartridge — NSF-61 pipe, nitrogen flushed, desiccant inside, RFID tag in wall, conical bottom, two one-way valves (outlet valve + cone inlet check valve)
  • Outlet valve — one-way silicone, closes when cartridge removed, first powder barrier
  • Falcon auger in sealed 316 SS tube — stepper motor, quadrature encoder verified, load cell weight confirmed ±2g
  • Cone inlet check valve — spring-loaded stainless, second powder barrier, calibrated against CIP steam pressure AND lightest powder (caffeine)
  • Receiving cone — wide top 10–12", 30–45° taper, 32 Ra electropolished, 360° spray ball at apex, ice inlet on wall
  • Blend cylinder — 5" ID, 8–10" tall, Silverson BE275 rotor/stator, SPX Flow shaft seal, PowerFlex 525 VFD
  • Pinch valve — 1.5" silicone, Plast-O-Matic, CO₂ assist 8–10 PSI
  • Dispense station — electric strike lock (fail-safe open), 12-key keypad, manual push-button override, LED status
CIP Sequence — After Every Order
  • 360° spray ball — hot water flush manifold and cone surfaces
  • Steam pulse — 212°F, 10 seconds
  • UV-C exposure — 15 seconds through quartz port
  • Air purge — until humidity sensor confirms dry
  • Final cold rinse
  • Extended CIP at 11pm and 4:30am daily
Off-Hours Protocol — 11pm to 5am
  • Kiosk screen off
  • Ice maker powered down
  • Extended CIP cycle at 11pm
  • Extended CIP cycle + drying at 4:30am
  • PLC remains active — monitoring and logging
  • Ice maker restarts at 4:30am
  • Full self-test at 4:50am before 5am open

BLEND+ · Operations
99.5% uptime target.
155 min/month budget.
Operating hours 5am–10pm = 510 hours/month. 99.5% uptime = maximum 155 minutes of downtime per month across all causes.
Preventive Maintenance Schedule
ComponentIntervalAction
Spray ballMonthlyInspect, clean if needed — keep 2 spares per vehicle
Silicone gasketsMonthly PM visitVisual inspection, replace if worn
Connecting tubes × 12QuarterlyReplace full set with pre-cleaned HQ inventory, return soiled set
Iris valve sleeveAnnuallyReplace regardless of condition — $15–$40
Pinch valve silicone tubeAnnuallyReplace regardless of condition — $5–$20
Shaft seal (SPX Flow)AnnuallyReplace proactively — never run to failure
Check valve springs × 12AnnuallyReplace springs — $5–$15 each
Food-grade tubingEvery 2 yearsReplace all food-contact tubing
UV-C lamp18–24 monthsReplace at 7,500 PLC-tracked hours — never wait for failure
Silverson workhead3–5 yearsInspect gap, replace if worn — field-swappable
Ice maker full serviceAnnuallyManufacturer PM protocol
Ice maker replacementYear 8–10Capital replacement — budget in financial model
Top 5 Failure Modes by Risk
FailureRiskMitigation
Check valve fails open under CIP steamCriticalValidate spring force vs steam pressure before Machine 1 ships
Shaft seal failureCriticalReplace annually — never run to failure
Electric strike lock failureHighFail-safe open spec + manual release + spare on vehicle
RFID read lockoutHighAlert + operator override — never hard lockout
VFD failureHighCarry spare PowerFlex 525 on every service vehicle
BLEND+ · Software Stack
App built. Dashboard built.
Machine integration next.
Consumer app and operator dashboard are live. PLC middleware and kiosk interface are the remaining build items. Water Innovations handles the full machine-side stack including cloud connectivity.
Consumer App — Live
  • React Native / Expo — iOS first
  • Supabase backend — auth, database, real-time
  • Stripe — payments, Apple Pay, sandbox → live pending
  • Twilio A2P — phone auth pending (2–6 week registration)
  • Tier system — Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum active
  • Silver trial — 7 days free for new members
  • 8 active rewards — all placeholder redemption
  • Happy Hour — timezone fix deployed
Operator Dashboard — Live
  • Standalone HTML — no server required
  • KPI cards with MoM comparison and projected payout
  • Hourly distribution — strongest/weakest hours
  • Day of week chart — best/slowest days
  • 30-day trend — orders and revenue dual axis
  • Member stats — rating, repeat rate, avg orders
  • Machine operations — ingredient levels, CO₂, cups
  • Connection to Supabase tables — pending Machine 1
PLC + Machine Stack — Pending
  • Allen-Bradley CompactLogix — Cooper Ward
  • Studio 5000 ladder logic — state machine architecture
  • EtherNet/IP — PLC to kiosk and cloud
  • Water Innovations cloud layer — existing capability
  • Order status contract — paid→blending→ready→picked_up
  • Self-test sequence on every boot
  • Off-hours schedule — 11pm to 5am protocol
Production Checklist — Key Items
  • Twilio A2P registration — start 2 months before launch
  • Remove DEV_AUTH_FALLBACK_ENABLED — 7 screens
  • Remove DEV_BYPASS_PAYMENT
  • Stripe live keys — replace test keys
  • Drop 3 dev RLS policies from Supabase
  • Account deletion — Apple requirement
  • Apple Pay merchant ID mismatch — fix before submission
BLEND+ · Market Size
Every location where people
care about what they put in their body.
BLEND+ is not a gym machine. It is a fresh nutrition platform that deploys wherever a health-conscious captive audience exists. The addressable universe is larger than any single vertical suggests.
Total Addressable Market — US Only
Gyms + College Campuses + Clinical Wellness + Corporate + Airports + Sporting Facilities + Senior Living + Sports Teams = 500,000+ viable placement locations in the United States alone. At one machine per location and $9,200/month gross per machine, the US TAM exceeds $55 billion in annual gross revenue at full penetration. BLEND+ does not need to capture 1% of this to be a meaningful acquisition target.
Vertical 1 — Gyms & Fitness Centers · Primary Market
Total US gyms
107K
IBISWorld 2026
Total members
77M
HFA 2024 record high
Industry revenue
$46B
2025 estimate
BLEND+ target
~40K
Mid-to-large facilities
The primary and immediate market. Gyms have the highest member density, the strongest nutritional motivation (members are actively working out), and existing comfort with premium wellness products. Planet Fitness (2,732 locations), Anytime Fitness (2,328), CrossFit (3,806), Orangetheory (1,269), LA Fitness (639), F45 (730) are all individually significant chains. Mid-size and independent gyms represent the long tail. The SmartFit relationship (Scottsdale Stage 2) demonstrates that multi-location gym investors are ready buyers of the model. JL Marketing's network of hundreds of gym clients is a warm introduction pipeline into this vertical.
Vertical 2 — College & University Campuses
4-year institutions
~4,000
US colleges + universities
Total enrollment
19M
Students annually
Avg campus rec center
2–5
Fitness facilities per campus
BLEND+ target
~8,000
Placement locations
College students are the highest-density nutrition market in existence — they live on campus, they work out on campus, they eat on campus, and they are highly receptive to new food and beverage technology. Campus rec centers are already high-traffic locations with built-in populations. The BLEND+ app loyalty system is perfectly suited for the college demographic — repeat daily behavior, digital-native users, heavy social sharing. A single flagship university partnership creates a proof point for the entire higher education vertical.
Vertical 3 — Clinical Wellness & Longevity Clinics
Longevity clinics US
2,000+
Growing 15%/yr
Med spas + wellness
8,000+
US locations
Avg client spend
$3K+
Per year on wellness
BLEND+ target
~5,000
Premium facilities
The Lumati partnership in Encinitas is the proof of concept for this vertical. Clinical wellness facilities serve clients who are already spending thousands on longevity optimization — peptide therapy, IV drips, hormone panels, cryotherapy. A BLEND+ machine with the Lumati Gold formula in Hopper 11 is a natural extension of the facility's service offering, not an add-on. The clinical wellness client has the highest willingness to pay and the strongest motivation to consume functional nutrition consistently. Average order value in this vertical is likely $10–$15 versus the $7 gym average.
Vertical 4 — Corporate Campuses
Fortune 500 campuses
500+
Primary HQ facilities
Corp wellness market
$68B
US 2025 estimate
Campuses w/ gyms
~15,000
Mid-large employers
BLEND+ target
~10,000
Employer-funded placements
Corporate wellness is one of the fastest-growing employee benefit categories. Companies like Google, Apple, Meta, and thousands of mid-size employers have on-site fitness centers, wellness programs, and nutrition benefits. A BLEND+ machine in a corporate gym is a premium employee benefit that a company might subsidize — reducing effective member cost to $0 and driving order volume through the roof. The employer-funded placement model changes the revenue equation significantly. This vertical requires a slightly different sales approach — campus facilities management rather than gym operators — but the host economics are the same.
Vertical 5 — Airports
Commercial airports
500
FAA commercial service
Major hub airports
~75
High traffic primaries
Annual passengers
900M
US domestic 2024
BLEND+ target
~200
Major + mid-size airports
Airports are one of the highest-revenue food and beverage locations in the world. Captive audiences, long dwell times, and elevated price tolerance make airports a premium placement opportunity. A traveler looking for a protein shake or a clean pre-flight nutrition hit has almost no good options today — airport food is notoriously poor quality. BLEND+ at $7–$10 in an airport setting is cheaper than the $14 protein bar at Hudson News and dramatically higher quality. The regulatory and infrastructure requirements for airport placement are more complex than gyms — this is a Phase 3+ vertical, not Phase 1.
Vertical 6 — Sporting Facilities & Professional Teams
Pro sports teams US
150+
Across NFL/NBA/MLB/NHL/MLS
College athletic programs
1,100+
NCAA D1/D2/D3
Sports training facilities
5,000+
Private + institutional
BLEND+ target
~3,000
Team + training facilities
Professional and collegiate sports organizations are among the most nutrition-focused institutions in the world. Players need precise post-workout nutrition, and training staff need to control exactly what athletes consume. A BLEND+ machine in a professional team's training facility is a premium nutrition tool — not a vending machine. The placement model changes here: the team is both the host and potentially a branded partner. An NFL team with a BLEND+ machine running their branded protein formula is a marketing story that sells machines to every gym with that team's fans. The brand halo from one pro team partnership is significant.
Vertical 7 — Senior Living & Retirement Communities
Assisted living facilities
30,500+
US 2026
Independent living communities
~5,000
55+ active communities
Residents
800K+
In assisted living alone
BLEND+ target
~8,000
Active + independent living
The senior living vertical requires a different product configuration — lower caffeine options, collagen, magnesium, electrolytes, and the Lumati Gold clinical formula are all directly relevant. Independent living and active adult communities (55+) are the most viable — residents are mobile, health-conscious, and have significant disposable income. The Lumati partnership creates a natural bridge into the clinical senior wellness space. This vertical grows at 4%+ annually with the aging baby boomer population and represents a long-duration placement — a machine in a senior living community stays for years, not months.

The Penetration Math
BLEND+ needs to reach 500 locations — 0.67% of the Phase 1+2 target alone — to hit $55M in annual fleet gross revenue at Year 5. This is not a market size problem. It is a distribution and trust problem. JL Marketing's gym network, the SmartFit anchor, and the Lumati clinical partnership are all distribution solutions, not marketing solutions. The machine sells itself when operators see the dashboard.
BLEND+ · TAM Penetration
What 1%, 2.5%, and 5%
of the market looks like.
BLEND+ is not a gym machine. It is a fresh nutrition platform that deploys wherever a health-conscious captive audience exists. The addressable universe is 74,200 viable placement locations across seven verticals in the US alone. These projections model what modest, achievable penetration of that universe produces.
Two Customer Archetypes — Different Markets, Different Mechanics
Captive + Recurring
The audience returns every day by default. BLEND+ becomes part of their routine — not a discovery. Loyalty mechanics, streaks, and tier systems compound over time. Revenue is predictable because the audience is structurally locked in.
GymsWellness CentersCollege CampusesCorporateSenior Living
Phase 1 + 2 priority · App-driven retention · $7–$10 AOV
Captive + Transient
The audience is stuck somewhere and needs quality nutrition that isn't available elsewhere. High traffic, low individual repeat rate. Revenue depends on foot traffic volume. Different machine configuration, different host pitch, different price point.
AirportsTransit HubsSporting VenuesHospital Lobbies
Phase 3 priority · Volume-driven · $10–$15 AOV

742 machines represents capturing 1 in every 100 viable locations. At the JL Marketing network scale alone — hundreds of gym clients nationally — 742 introductions is a realistic ask over 5–6 years with the right distribution partnerships in place. At this scale BLEND+ is a meaningful acquisition target generating nearly $100M in annual gross revenue across a defensible host network.

1,855 machines is the most likely acquisition trigger scenario. At nearly $250M in annual fleet gross revenue and $197M BLEND+ net, the business has the scale, the data, and the host network density that makes it a category-defining asset for a Glanbia, a gym chain rollup, or a private equity platform. A supplement company acquiring at a 3x revenue multiple pays $590M for a business that cost $275K in seed capital to prove.

5% penetration is the standalone scale scenario — what BLEND+ looks like if it is never acquired and continues to grow independently. Nearly $500M in annual fleet gross, $395M BLEND+ net, and a host network of 3,710 locations across gyms, campuses, wellness centers, and beyond. At this scale the business is a platform, not a product — and the conversation with an acquirer is about buying a category leader, not a machine company.

Go-To-Market Sequence — Attacking Markets in Order of Fit
PHASE 1
Gyms · Wellness Centers · Clinical Facilities
Highest product-market fit. Recurring captive audience with strong nutritional motivation. App loyalty mechanics deliver maximum retention value. JL Marketing warm introductions, SmartFit anchor, Lumati clinical partnership. Message: "Zero cost placement, guaranteed monthly revenue, built-in member retention tool."
PHASE 2
College Campuses · Corporate Campuses · Senior Living
Strong recurring captive audiences with different decision-making structures. Campus: facilities management and student life departments. Corporate: employee benefits and wellness program managers. Senior living: activity directors and operations teams. Message adapts per audience — student wellness, employee benefit, or resident nutrition. App mechanics adapt to each demographic — college members respond to streaks and leaderboards; corporate members respond to convenience and personalization; senior members respond to health credentials and ease of use.
PHASE 3
Airports · Sporting Facilities · Transit Hubs
Transient captive audience — different machine configuration, higher AOV, volume-dependent revenue. Requires different infrastructure relationships (airport concession agreements, venue contracts). Machine may require kiosk-only ordering without app dependency. Revenue is driven by foot traffic density, not member loyalty. Approached only after the recurring captive model is proven and the brand is established enough to command premium placement fees in high-traffic venues.
The Honest Investor Framing
None of these scenarios require BLEND+ to be the dominant player in any vertical. 1% penetration of the gym market alone — 1,070 gyms out of 107,000 — generates nearly $100M in annual fleet gross. The market size is not the question. The question is whether the machine works, whether hosts trust the model, and whether the unit economics hold at scale. Machine 1 answers all three. That is what this round is funding.
BLEND+ · Growth & Retention
Every mechanic drives
one behavior — come back.
The app is not a payment interface. It is a retention engine. Every feature is designed to make the next visit more likely than the last. The machine sells the first drink. The app sells every drink after that.
Tier System — The Core Retention Architecture
TierUnlock ThresholdBB Earn RatePerks
BronzeDefault1xStandard pricing, standard earn rate
Silver5 purchases or trial1.25xPriority queue, early Happy Hour access, 7-day free trial for new members
Gold20 purchases1.5xMonthly free drink, exclusive flavor access, referral bonus multiplier
Platinum50 purchases2xCustom blend save slots, early rotating flavor access, Blend Bucks gifting
Silver trial is deployed for every new member — they experience Silver perks immediately, making the downgrade to Bronze feel like a loss rather than the baseline. This is a deliberate retention mechanic borrowed from consumer subscription psychology. Members who experience Silver are significantly more likely to complete the 5 purchases needed to earn it permanently.
Blend Bucks — The Currency That Drives Frequency
Earning BB
  • Every purchase earns BB at tier-adjusted rate
  • Bonus BB Day — 400 BB for ordering on a designated day
  • Streak maintenance — BB multiplier for consecutive-day visits
  • Referral — BB for referring a friend who completes their first order
  • Rating a drink — small BB reward drives review behavior
Redeeming BB — 8 Active Rewards
  • Free Supplement Add-On — 150 BB
  • Double Scoop Upgrade — 250 BB
  • Happy Hour access — 150 BB
  • Streak Shield — protects streak on a missed day (scaled cost)
  • Bonus BB Day — 400 BB
  • Surprise Me Blend — 300 BB (machine chooses)
  • Free Drink — 1,000 BB
  • Gift 500 BB to a Friend — 200 BB
Happy Hour — Dynamic Pricing That Moves Volume
Happy Hour is a configurable pricing window set per location from the operator dashboard. During Happy Hour, selected drinks are discounted — typically 20%. The mechanic serves two purposes simultaneously: it drives volume during off-peak hours (reducing the gap between the 44 order base case and the 70 order strong case), and it creates urgency — a member who knows Happy Hour ends at 10am is more likely to order before 10am than to skip the gym entirely.
Operator control
Set Happy Hour window and discount from the dashboard. Adjust per day of week. Configurable without a service visit.
Member behavior
Push notification sent 15 minutes before Happy Hour starts. Members who opt in arrive earlier, order more, and visit more consistently.
Revenue impact
A 20% discount during a 2-hour window that adds 8 incremental orders per day adds $1,680/month at full price equivalent. The volume more than offsets the margin concession.
Streak System — Daily Habit Formation
A streak is the number of consecutive days a member orders from BLEND+. Streaks are displayed prominently on the home screen — a visible counter that creates loss aversion. Missing a day resets the streak, which is a psychologically significant loss for a member who has maintained a 14-day run.
  • Streak display — prominent on home screen, shown as a flame icon with day count. Makes the streak feel like an achievement worth protecting.
  • Streak Shield — purchasable with Blend Bucks, protects the streak for one missed day. Creates a secondary spend behavior and softens churn from travel or illness.
  • Streak milestones — bonus BB awards at 7, 14, 30, and 60-day streaks. Each milestone is a push notification moment — drives social sharing behavior.
  • Streak leaderboard — optional, per-location. Members can see where they rank among their gym's BLEND+ users. Competitive environments (CrossFit gyms, college teams) respond strongly to leaderboard mechanics.
Referral Engine — Organic Acquisition
Every member who has a good experience is a potential acquisition channel. The referral mechanic turns satisfied members into a distribution network — which is how BLEND+ grows within a location without any additional marketing spend.
Member A sends invite
Shares a unique referral link from the app. Earns BB when their friend completes their first order.
Member B joins
Downloads app via referral link. Automatically enrolled in Silver trial. First order confirmed.
Both earn
Member A earns BB reward. Member B starts their Silver trial. Two retention clocks start simultaneously.
Surprise Me Blend — Delight and Discovery
A member spends 300 BB to let the machine choose their drink — a random combination from their flavor profile and past order history. The mechanic serves three purposes: it burns accumulated BB (preventing BB hoarding that reduces redemption pressure), it introduces members to flavor combinations they might not have tried, and it creates a shareable moment — members post their Surprise Me results. The social sharing of a Surprise Me result is free marketing at the gym level.
Push Notification Strategy — Timed Triggers
TriggerTimingMessageGoal
Happy Hour start15 min before"Happy Hour starts in 15 — 20% off until 10am"Drive off-peak volume
Streak at risk4pm if no order"Your 12-day streak ends tonight — grab your blend"Prevent churn
Milestone reachedInstant"30-day streak 🔥 — you've earned 500 bonus BB"Drive sharing
New rotating flavorLaunch day"New flavor just dropped — [Name] is in Hopper 7"Re-engage lapsed users
Low BB warningBelow 100 BB"Only 150 BB to your next free add-on — order today"Drive frequency
Referral completedInstant"[Friend] just ordered — you've earned 200 BB"Reinforce referral behavior
Silver tier expiry3 days before"Your Silver status expires in 3 days — 1 order locks it in"Prevent tier downgrade churn
Saved Blends — Platinum Feature, Habit Lock-In
Platinum members can save up to 5 custom blend configurations — their exact protein choice, base, supplement stack, and caffeine dose — as one-tap orders. This reduces friction to near-zero for a habitual user. When ordering takes 3 seconds instead of 30, the decision to skip becomes harder to justify. Saved blends also create switching costs — a member with 5 saved blends at a BLEND+ machine has a meaningful reason to stay with the machine over a competitor.
Gift Blend Bucks — Social Spread
Members can gift 500 BB to a friend for 200 BB — a mechanism that introduces non-members to the BLEND+ ecosystem. The recipient gets 500 BB — enough for a meaningful reward — and is prompted to download the app to claim them. The gifter spends 200 BB to generate a warm lead who is already financially committed to trying BLEND+. This is the lowest-cost acquisition channel in the system.
The Retention Flywheel
First drink → Silver trial starts → streak begins → Blend Bucks accumulate → first reward redeemed → tier progresses → saved blends lock in → referral sent → new member joins. Each step in this sequence makes the next visit more likely. A member at Platinum with 3 saved blends and a 45-day streak is not going to stop coming because the machine is slightly out of the way. That is the product we are building.
